name: Cockroach Extermination
description: Professional cockroach extermination services for homes and businesses across NYC.
extendedDescription: NYC's dense housing and restaurant scene make cockroach infestations one of the most common pest problems in the metro area. German cockroaches thrive in kitchens and bathrooms, while American cockroaches invade basements and utility areas. Professional extermination combines gel baits, IGR treatments, and crack-and-crevice applications for lasting results.

faqs.q: How much does cockroach extermination cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Cockroach extermination in NYC typically costs $150-$400 for a single treatment, or $30-$60/month for ongoing maintenance plans. Multi-unit buildings and severe infestations may cost more. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing.

faqs.q: How long does cockroach treatment take to work?
faqs.a: You'll see a significant reduction in cockroach activity within 1-2 weeks after treatment. Complete elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks with follow-up treatments. Severe infestations in multi-unit buildings may require 4-6 weeks.

faqs.q: Are cockroach treatments safe for pets and children?
faqs.a: Yes. We use EPA-approved products and targeted application methods that minimize exposure. Gel baits are placed in cracks and crevices away from contact areas. We'll provide specific safety instructions for your household.

faqs.q: Why do cockroaches keep coming back?
faqs.a: In NYC, cockroaches often re-enter from neighboring units, building infrastructure, or deliveries. That's why we recommend ongoing maintenance plans and work with building management for comprehensive treatment.

faqs.q: Do you treat restaurants and commercial kitchens for cockroaches?
faqs.a: Yes. We specialize in NYC DOH-compliant commercial cockroach treatments for restaurants, bakeries, and food service businesses. We work around your schedule and provide documentation for health inspections.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Bed Bug Treatment
description: Expert bed bug detection and elimination using heat treatment and targeted chemical applications.
extendedDescription: Bed bugs are one of NYC's most persistent pest problems, affecting apartments, hotels, and offices across all five boroughs. Professional bed bug treatment requires thorough inspection, often with K-9 detection, followed by heat treatment or targeted chemical applications. NYC's Bed Bug Disclosure Act requires landlords to disclose infestation history.

faqs.q: How much does bed bug treatment cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Bed bug treatment in NYC ranges from $300-$1,500 per room depending on the method. Heat treatment is $1,000-$1,500 per room. Chemical treatment starts at $300-$600 per room. Multi-room discounts are available.

faqs.q: Is heat treatment or chemical treatment better for bed bugs?
faqs.a: Heat treatment kills all life stages in a single day and is chemical-free. Chemical treatment costs less but requires 2-3 follow-up visits. We recommend heat treatment for severe infestations and chemical treatment for early-stage problems.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have bed bugs?
faqs.a: Signs include small red bite marks in lines or clusters, tiny dark spots on sheets (fecal stains), shed skins near mattress seams, and a sweet musty odor. We offer free inspections and K-9 detection for definitive confirmation.

faqs.q: Can my landlord be responsible for bed bug treatment costs?
faqs.a: In NYC, landlords are generally required to pay for bed bug extermination in rental apartments under the NYC Housing Maintenance Code. We can provide documentation to support your case.

faqs.q: How long after treatment until bed bugs are gone?
faqs.a: Heat treatment eliminates bed bugs the same day. Chemical treatment typically requires 2-3 treatments over 3-4 weeks to ensure all eggs have hatched and been treated. We guarantee our work with follow-up inspections.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses. K-9 teams are certified.

name: Rat Extermination
description: Comprehensive rat control including extermination, exclusion, and prevention for NYC properties.
extendedDescription: NYC's rat population is legendary, and the city's ongoing rat mitigation efforts make professional extermination essential. Norway rats dominate subway tunnels, basements, and outdoor burrows, while roof rats target upper floors and attics. Effective rat control combines baiting, trapping, exclusion (sealing entry points), and sanitation consulting.

faqs.q: How much does rat extermination cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Rat extermination in NYC typically costs $200-$600 for initial treatment, plus $50-$100/month for ongoing monitoring. Exclusion work (sealing entry points) ranges from $500-$2,000 depending on the property. We provide free inspections.

faqs.q: How do rats get into NYC apartments?
faqs.a: Rats enter through gaps as small as a quarter around pipes, utility lines, door sweeps, and foundation cracks. They also travel through connected basements and building infrastructure. Our exclusion service identifies and seals all entry points.

faqs.q: Are NYC landlords required to provide rat extermination?
faqs.a: Yes. NYC landlords are legally required to keep rental properties free of rats and other vermin under the Housing Maintenance Code. Tenants can call 311 to report violations. We work with both landlords and property managers.

faqs.q: What is NYC's rat mitigation zone program?
faqs.a: NYC has designated rat mitigation zones where property owners must comply with specific rat prevention measures. We help property owners in these zones meet all requirements including inspections, baiting, and documentation.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get rid of rats?
faqs.a: Initial rat population reduction is typically achieved within 1-2 weeks. Complete elimination with exclusion work takes 3-6 weeks. We recommend ongoing monitoring to prevent re-infestation, especially in high-density NYC neighborhoods.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Mouse Extermination
description: Professional mouse control services including trapping, baiting, and exclusion for NYC homes and businesses.
extendedDescription: House mice are among the most common pests in NYC apartments, restaurants, and commercial spaces. They can squeeze through gaps as small as a dime and reproduce rapidly. Professional mouse control combines strategic trapping, tamper-resistant bait stations, and thorough exclusion work to seal entry points and prevent re-infestation.

faqs.q: How much does mouse extermination cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Mouse extermination in NYC costs $150-$400 for initial treatment. Exclusion work to seal entry points adds $300-$1,500 depending on the property. Monthly monitoring plans run $40-$80/month.

faqs.q: What's the difference between mice and rats?
faqs.a: House mice are small (2-4 inches), light brown/gray, with large ears relative to body size. Rats are much larger (7-10 inches), brown or black, with thicker tails. Treatment approaches differ — mice respond better to traps while rats often require baiting programs.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have mice?
faqs.a: Signs include small dark droppings (rice-sized), gnaw marks on food packaging, scratching sounds in walls at night, and greasy rub marks along baseboards. We offer free inspections to confirm and assess the severity.

faqs.q: Can one mouse mean an infestation?
faqs.a: Usually yes. If you see one mouse, there are likely more. Mice are social and breed quickly — a single pair can produce 60+ offspring per year. Early professional treatment prevents a small problem from becoming a major infestation.

faqs.q: Are mouse traps or poison better?
faqs.a: We typically recommend traps for residential settings — they're safer around children and pets, and you can confirm catches. Bait stations are more effective for large commercial spaces and severe infestations. We'll recommend the right approach for your situation.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Ant Control
description: Effective ant control and elimination for homes and businesses, targeting all NYC ant species.
extendedDescription: Ant infestations in NYC range from nuisance pavement ants invading kitchens to destructive carpenter ants damaging wood structures. Odorous house ants, pharaoh ants, and citronella ants are also common. Effective ant control requires identifying the species, locating the colony, and using targeted baits that workers carry back to eliminate the queen.

faqs.q: How much does ant control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Ant control in NYC costs $150-$350 for standard treatment. Carpenter ant treatment costs more ($300-$600) due to the need for wall void treatment and wood damage assessment. Ongoing prevention plans run $30-$50/month.

faqs.q: Why do I have ants in my NYC apartment?
faqs.a: Ants enter NYC apartments seeking food and water, especially in spring and summer. They follow scent trails through tiny cracks around windows, doors, and utility penetrations. Keeping surfaces clean helps, but professional treatment is needed to eliminate colonies.

faqs.q: How long does ant treatment take to work?
faqs.a: Bait treatments take 1-2 weeks to eliminate the colony as workers carry poison back to the queen. You may see increased ant activity initially — that's a good sign, meaning ants are feeding on the bait. Complete elimination typically occurs within 2-3 weeks.

faqs.q: Are carpenter ants common in NYC?
faqs.a: Yes, especially in brownstones, townhouses, and wood-frame buildings. Carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it for nesting, causing structural damage. Signs include sawdust piles and large black ants. Early treatment prevents costly repairs.

faqs.q: Do you offer ant prevention programs?
faqs.a: Yes. Our seasonal ant prevention includes perimeter treatments in spring and summer, entry point sealing, and quarterly inspections. Prevention is far less expensive than dealing with a full infestation.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Spider Control
description: Professional spider removal and prevention services for NYC homes and businesses.
extendedDescription: While most NYC spiders are harmless, heavy spider activity indicates other pest problems since spiders feed on insects. Common species include house spiders, cellar spiders, and occasionally brown recluses in storage areas. Professional spider control addresses both the spiders and the underlying insect prey population.

faqs.q: How much does spider control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Spider control in NYC costs $125-$300 for a standard treatment. This usually includes treating the underlying insect population that attracts spiders. Quarterly prevention plans run $30-$50/month.

faqs.q: Are there dangerous spiders in NYC?
faqs.a: NYC has very few dangerous spiders. Brown recluses are rare but occasionally found in storage areas and basements. Black widows are extremely rare in the city. Most NYC spiders — house spiders, cellar spiders — are harmless.

faqs.q: Why do I have so many spiders in my apartment?
faqs.a: Heavy spider activity usually means there's a significant insect population providing food. Addressing the underlying pest problem — ants, flies, gnats — often resolves spider issues. We treat both the spiders and their food source.

faqs.q: Do spider treatments prevent them from coming back?
faqs.a: Perimeter barrier treatments deter spiders for 60-90 days. Combined with reducing the insect prey population and sealing entry points, most clients see long-term reduction. Quarterly treatments maintain protection year-round.

faqs.q: Should I worry about spiders in my basement?
faqs.a: Cellar spiders (daddy longlegs) are very common in NYC basements and are harmless. However, excessive spider webs and activity can indicate moisture issues or other pest problems worth investigating.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Mosquito Control
description: Residential and commercial mosquito control including yard treatment and breeding site elimination.
extendedDescription: Mosquitoes are a seasonal but serious concern across the NYC metro area, carrying diseases like West Nile virus and Eastern Equine Encephalitis. Effective mosquito control targets breeding sites (standing water), applies barrier treatments to outdoor areas, and uses larvicides where needed. Properties with yards, patios, and outdoor dining areas benefit most.

faqs.q: How much does mosquito control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Mosquito control in NYC costs $75-$200 per treatment for residential properties. Seasonal programs (April-October) run $400-$800 for the season with treatments every 3-4 weeks. Commercial outdoor dining areas are quoted per square footage.

faqs.q: When is mosquito season in NYC?
faqs.a: Mosquito season in the NYC metro area runs from April through October, peaking in July and August. We recommend starting barrier treatments in April before populations build up.

faqs.q: Are mosquito barrier treatments safe for kids and pets?
faqs.a: Yes. Our barrier sprays use EPA-approved products that are safe once dry, typically within 30-60 minutes. We also offer natural/organic mosquito treatment options for families who prefer them.

faqs.q: Do mosquito treatments kill bees and butterflies?
faqs.a: We use targeted application methods that minimize impact on beneficial insects. Treatments are applied to resting areas (undersides of leaves, shaded areas) where mosquitoes hide, not to flowering plants where pollinators feed.

faqs.q: Can you do mosquito control for outdoor events?
faqs.a: Yes. We offer one-time event mosquito treatments for weddings, parties, and outdoor gatherings. Treatment is applied 24-48 hours before your event for maximum effectiveness.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Flea Treatment
description: Professional flea elimination for homes with pets, including treatment and prevention programs.
extendedDescription: Flea infestations in NYC homes typically originate from pets but can persist even in pet-free environments. Fleas breed rapidly — a single flea can produce 50 eggs per day. Effective treatment requires addressing adult fleas, larvae, pupae, and eggs across all living areas, pet bedding, and upholstered furniture.

faqs.q: How much does flea treatment cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Professional flea treatment in NYC costs $200-$400 for a standard home treatment. Most infestations require 2-3 treatments spaced 2 weeks apart to break the breeding cycle. The total cost is typically $400-$800.

faqs.q: How long does flea treatment take to work?
faqs.a: You'll see reduced flea activity within 24-48 hours after treatment. However, flea pupae can remain dormant for weeks, so you may see some fleas emerge after initial treatment. Follow-up treatments 2 weeks later address these newly emerged fleas.

faqs.q: Do I need to treat my pet too?
faqs.a: Yes. Home treatment alone won't solve a flea problem if your pet is still carrying fleas. We recommend coordinating with your veterinarian for pet flea treatment. We handle the home — your vet handles the pet.

faqs.q: Can fleas infest a home without pets?
faqs.a: Yes. Fleas can be brought in on clothing, infest wildlife-accessible areas, or persist from previous pet owners. Flea pupae can remain dormant for months and emerge when they detect vibrations or body heat.

faqs.q: How do I prepare my home for flea treatment?
faqs.a: Vacuum all floors and upholstery thoroughly, wash pet bedding in hot water, remove items from floors, and plan to be out of the home for 2-4 hours after treatment. We'll provide a detailed prep checklist.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Tick Control
description: Professional tick control and prevention for yards, properties, and outdoor areas across the NYC metro.
extendedDescription: Ticks carrying Lyme disease and other illnesses are an increasing concern across the NYC metro area, especially in suburban neighborhoods, parks, and properties bordering wooded areas. Deer ticks, lone star ticks, and dog ticks are all found in the region. Professional tick control combines yard treatment, habitat modification, and perimeter barriers.

faqs.q: How much does tick control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Tick control costs $100-$250 per treatment for residential properties. Seasonal programs (April-November) run $500-$1,000 with treatments every 4-6 weeks. Properties bordering wooded areas may need more frequent treatment.

faqs.q: When is tick season in NYC?
faqs.a: Tick season in the NYC metro area runs from April through November, with peak activity in May-July. Deer ticks can remain active in winter when temperatures are above freezing.

faqs.q: Are ticks a problem in NYC or just the suburbs?
faqs.a: Ticks are found throughout the NYC metro area, including city parks, backyards in outer boroughs, and suburban properties in NJ, Long Island, and Westchester. Staten Island and areas near green spaces have higher tick populations.

faqs.q: Do tick treatments protect against Lyme disease?
faqs.a: Professional tick treatment significantly reduces tick populations on your property, lowering the risk of Lyme disease and other tick-borne illnesses. Combined with personal prevention measures, yard treatment is one of the most effective protections.

faqs.q: Are tick treatments safe for pets?
faqs.a: Yes. Our tick treatments are safe for pets once dry, typically 30-60 minutes after application. We also coordinate with your tick prevention plan for pets (collars, topicals) for comprehensive protection.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Termite Treatment
description: Comprehensive termite inspection, treatment, and prevention for NYC metro properties.
extendedDescription: Eastern subterranean termites are the primary termite species in the NYC metro area, causing millions in property damage annually. They enter buildings through soil contact and can go undetected for years. Professional termite treatment includes thorough inspection, liquid barrier treatment or bait station systems, and ongoing monitoring to protect your investment.

faqs.q: How much does termite treatment cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Termite treatment in NYC costs $800-$3,000+ depending on property size and treatment method. Liquid barrier treatments run $800-$2,000. Bait station systems cost $1,500-$3,000 including installation and first year monitoring. Annual monitoring is $200-$400.

faqs.q: Are termites common in NYC?
faqs.a: Yes. Eastern subterranean termites are found throughout the NYC metro area. They're especially common in brownstones, wood-frame houses, and properties with wood-to-soil contact. Many infestations go undetected for years.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have termites?
faqs.a: Signs include mud tubes on foundation walls, hollow-sounding wood, discarded wings near windows (swarm season in spring), and bubbling or peeling paint. We offer free termite inspections to assess your property.

faqs.q: Do I need a termite inspection before buying a home?
faqs.a: Absolutely. A professional termite inspection (WDI report / NPMA-33) is essential before purchasing any property in the NYC metro area. We provide FHA/VA-compliant termite inspection letters for real estate transactions.

faqs.q: What's the difference between liquid treatment and bait stations?
faqs.a: Liquid barriers create a chemical barrier around your foundation that kills termites on contact. Bait stations use slow-acting toxins that workers carry back to the colony. Both are effective — we recommend based on your property's specific needs.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses with termite endorsement.

name: Carpenter Ant Control
description: Specialized carpenter ant detection and treatment to protect your property's wood structures.
extendedDescription: Carpenter ants are one of the most destructive pests in NYC-area homes, excavating galleries in wood to build nests. Unlike termites, they don't eat wood — they tunnel through it, leaving behind smooth galleries and piles of sawdust (frass). NYC brownstones, townhouses, and wood-frame homes are especially vulnerable. Treatment requires locating satellite colonies and the parent nest.

faqs.q: How much does carpenter ant treatment cost?
faqs.a: Carpenter ant treatment in NYC costs $300-$800 depending on the severity and number of colonies. Treatment often includes wall void injection and exterior barrier treatment. Follow-up visits are typically included.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have carpenter ants or regular ants?
faqs.a: Carpenter ants are large (1/4 to 1/2 inch), usually black, and you may find piles of fine sawdust (frass) near wooden structures. You might hear faint rustling sounds in walls. Regular ants are much smaller and don't produce sawdust.

faqs.q: Do carpenter ants cause as much damage as termites?
faqs.a: Carpenter ants cause significant structural damage but typically work slower than termites. However, a mature carpenter ant colony can have thousands of workers actively excavating wood. Left untreated, damage can be extensive and costly.

faqs.q: What attracts carpenter ants to my home?
faqs.a: Carpenter ants are attracted to moist, decaying wood. Water damage, roof leaks, plumbing issues, and wood-to-soil contact create ideal conditions. Addressing moisture problems is a key part of carpenter ant control.

faqs.q: How long does carpenter ant treatment take?
faqs.a: Initial treatment takes 1-2 hours. Colony elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks as bait is carried back to the nest. We include follow-up inspections at 2 and 4 weeks to ensure complete elimination.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Wasp Removal
description: Safe wasp nest removal and prevention for homes and businesses across NYC.
extendedDescription: Paper wasps, yellow jackets, and bald-faced hornets build nests on NYC buildings, in eaves, under decks, and in ground burrows. Wasp stings are painful and potentially dangerous for allergic individuals. Professional removal is essential for nests near high-traffic areas, entrances, and outdoor dining spaces. We remove the nest and apply preventive treatments.

faqs.q: How much does wasp removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Wasp nest removal in NYC costs $100-$350 depending on nest location and accessibility. Ground nests and hard-to-reach locations cost more. Emergency same-day service may carry an additional fee.

faqs.q: Is it safe to remove a wasp nest myself?
faqs.a: We strongly advise against DIY wasp nest removal. Disturbed wasps become aggressive and can sting multiple times. Professional removal uses protective equipment and targeted treatments to minimize risk. This is especially important for anyone with sting allergies.

faqs.q: When should I have a wasp nest removed?
faqs.a: The best time is early spring when nests are small and colonies are just starting. However, we remove nests year-round. Nests near entrances, walkways, or outdoor dining areas should be removed immediately regardless of size.

faqs.q: Will wasps return after nest removal?
faqs.a: After professional removal, we treat the area with residual products that deter wasps from rebuilding in the same spot. Without treatment, wasps often do return to the same location. Our treatment prevents this.

faqs.q: What's the difference between wasps, hornets, and yellow jackets?
faqs.a: Paper wasps build open umbrella-shaped nests, often under eaves. Hornets build large enclosed nests in trees or on buildings. Yellow jackets often nest underground or in wall voids and are the most aggressive. All are treated differently.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Bee Removal
description: Humane bee removal and relocation services for homes and commercial properties.
extendedDescription: Honeybees, bumblebees, and carpenter bees are common across NYC. While honeybees and bumblebees are beneficial pollinators, nests in walls, eaves, or high-traffic areas pose safety risks. We prioritize humane live removal and relocation when possible. Carpenter bees, which bore into wood, require different treatment to prevent structural damage.

faqs.q: How much does bee removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Bee removal in NYC costs $150-$500 depending on nest location and type. Live honeybee removal and relocation typically costs $200-$500. Carpenter bee treatment runs $150-$350. Wall void removals requiring construction access cost more.

faqs.q: Do you kill bees or relocate them?
faqs.a: We prioritize live removal and relocation for honeybees and bumblebees whenever possible. We work with local beekeepers to rehome colonies. Carpenter bees, which damage wood structures, are treated differently to prevent further damage.

faqs.q: Are carpenter bees damaging my home?
faqs.a: Yes. Carpenter bees bore perfectly round 1/2-inch holes into wood surfaces — decks, fascia boards, eaves, and siding. While individual holes seem minor, repeated nesting year after year causes cumulative structural damage.

faqs.q: What should I do if I see a bee swarm?
faqs.a: Stay calm and keep your distance. Swarming honeybees are generally docile as they're focused on finding a new home. Call us for safe swarm removal — we'll relocate them to a beekeeper within hours.

faqs.q: Is it legal to kill honeybees in NYC?
faqs.a: NYC has no specific law against killing honeybees, but beekeeping is legal and the city encourages pollinator protection. We always attempt humane relocation first. It's the responsible approach and our clients expect it.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses. Bee relocation done in partnership with licensed NYC beekeepers.

name: Hornet Removal
description: Professional hornet nest removal for homes and businesses, including bald-faced hornets.
extendedDescription: Bald-faced hornets and European hornets are common in the NYC metro area, building large paper nests in trees, on buildings, and under eaves. Hornets are aggressive defenders of their nests and can sting multiple times. Professional removal is essential, especially for nests near entrances, walkways, and outdoor spaces.

faqs.q: How much does hornet removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Hornet nest removal in NYC costs $150-$400 depending on nest size and location. Hard-to-reach nests requiring ladder work or bucket trucks cost more. Same-day emergency removal may carry an additional fee.

faqs.q: Are hornets more dangerous than wasps?
faqs.a: Hornets are generally more aggressive when their nest is disturbed and their stings are more painful due to higher venom concentration. Bald-faced hornets are especially defensive and will chase perceived threats. Professional removal is strongly recommended.

faqs.q: When are hornets most active?
faqs.a: Hornets are most active from late spring through fall. Colonies peak in size during August and September. Nests are abandoned in winter, but new queens may build near the same location the following spring.

faqs.q: Can hornets damage my house?
faqs.a: Hornets don't typically cause structural damage, but their large nests (sometimes basketball-sized) can stain siding, clog gutters, and create safety hazards near entrances. Early removal prevents nests from growing.

faqs.q: Should I remove a hornet nest in winter?
faqs.a: Hornet nests are abandoned in late fall/winter and the colony dies off. You can safely remove the empty nest yourself at that point. However, treating the area prevents new queens from building there in spring.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.
competitorSigns: Competitors ranking for hornet removal searches

name: Yellow Jacket Removal
description: Expert yellow jacket nest removal for ground nests, wall voids, and outdoor areas.
extendedDescription: Yellow jackets are among the most aggressive stinging insects in the NYC metro area. They commonly build nests underground, in wall voids, and under structures. Yellow jackets are responsible for most stinging insect injuries and can be life-threatening for allergic individuals. Ground nest removal is especially dangerous without professional equipment.

faqs.q: How much does yellow jacket removal cost?
faqs.a: Yellow jacket removal in NYC costs $125-$350 depending on nest location. Ground nests are typically $125-$200. Wall void nests requiring access cost $200-$350. Emergency same-day service may carry an additional fee.

faqs.q: Why are yellow jackets so aggressive?
faqs.a: Yellow jackets are social wasps that aggressively defend their nest. They can sting multiple times and release alarm pheromones that recruit more workers to attack. They become especially aggressive in late summer when colonies are at peak size.

faqs.q: How do I find a yellow jacket ground nest?
faqs.a: Watch for yellow jackets entering and exiting a small hole in the ground, usually in a sunny area of the yard. The entrance hole is typically 1-2 inches across. Do not disturb it — ground nests can contain thousands of workers.

faqs.q: Can yellow jacket stings be dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes. Yellow jacket stings cause severe pain and swelling in everyone, but can trigger life-threatening anaphylaxis in allergic individuals. Multiple stings from a disturbed nest can be dangerous for anyone. Seek medical attention for severe reactions.

faqs.q: When are yellow jackets most active?
faqs.a: Yellow jackets are most active and aggressive from August through October when colonies are largest. They're attracted to sugary foods and meat at outdoor gatherings. Early-season nest removal prevents late-summer problems.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.
seoChecklist: Optimize GBP for yellow jacket removal
competitorSigns: Competitors ranking for yellow jacket searches

name: Raccoon Removal
description: Humane raccoon trapping, removal, and exclusion for NYC metro properties.
extendedDescription: Raccoons are increasingly common across NYC, living in attics, crawl spaces, chimneys, and under decks. They carry rabies, roundworm, and other diseases. NYC law requires humane trapping and release or euthanasia by licensed wildlife control operators. DIY removal is dangerous and often illegal. Professional exclusion prevents re-entry.

faqs.q: How much does raccoon removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Raccoon removal in NYC costs $300-$800 depending on location and number of animals. Attic raccoons with babies cost more due to careful removal requirements. Exclusion and entry point sealing adds $500-$2,000.

faqs.q: Is it legal to trap raccoons in NYC?
faqs.a: Raccoon trapping in NYC requires a Nuisance Wildlife Control Operator license from NYS DEC. All our technicians are properly licensed. DIY trapping is not recommended and may violate local regulations.

faqs.q: How do raccoons get into my attic?
faqs.a: Raccoons are strong and dexterous — they can rip open soffit vents, roof returns, and damaged areas to access attics. They often target homes in spring when females seek safe nesting sites for babies.

faqs.q: Are raccoons dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes. Raccoons can carry rabies, raccoon roundworm, and leptospirosis. They can be aggressive when cornered, especially mothers with babies. Never approach or attempt to handle a raccoon. Call a licensed professional.

faqs.q: What happens to the raccoon after removal?
faqs.a: NYS DEC regulations govern raccoon relocation. In most cases, raccoons are relocated to approved areas or humanely euthanized if they show signs of illness. We follow all state and local wildlife regulations.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Nuisance Wildlife Control Operator licenses.
competitorSigns: Competitors ranking for raccoon removal searches

name: Squirrel Removal
description: Professional squirrel removal and exclusion to protect your home from wildlife damage.
extendedDescription: Eastern gray squirrels frequently invade NYC attics, soffits, and wall voids, chewing through wiring, insulation, and wood. They pose fire hazards from gnawed electrical wires and create noise disturbances. Professional removal combines one-way exclusion doors, humane trapping, and thorough entry point sealing to prevent return.

faqs.q: How much does squirrel removal cost?
faqs.a: Squirrel removal in NYC costs $250-$600 for trapping and removal. One-way exclusion door installation costs $200-$400. Complete exclusion and entry point sealing adds $500-$1,500 depending on the property.

faqs.q: How do I know if squirrels are in my attic?
faqs.a: Signs include scratching and scurrying sounds during daytime (squirrels are diurnal, unlike rats), gnaw marks on wood and wires, droppings larger than rat droppings, and visible entry points along the roofline.

faqs.q: Can squirrels cause fires?
faqs.a: Yes. Squirrels frequently chew on electrical wiring in attics and walls, which is a leading cause of residential fires. If you suspect squirrels in your attic, have the wiring inspected as part of the removal process.

faqs.q: What's a one-way exclusion door?
faqs.a: A one-way door allows squirrels to exit your attic but prevents re-entry. It's the most humane removal method and avoids trapping animals inside. Once all squirrels have exited (usually 3-5 days), we seal the entry point permanently.

faqs.q: When are squirrels most likely to enter attics?
faqs.a: Squirrels seek attic access primarily in fall (preparing for winter) and late winter/early spring (breeding season). Pregnant females are especially determined to find warm, safe nesting sites.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Nuisance Wildlife Control Operator licenses.
seoChecklist: Build content about fire hazards from squirrels
competitorSigns: Competitors ranking for squirrel removal searches

name: Pigeon Control
description: Professional pigeon deterrent installation and cleanup for NYC buildings and properties.
extendedDescription: NYC's massive pigeon population creates health hazards and property damage on buildings, ledges, HVAC units, and signage. Pigeon droppings are corrosive, carry diseases including histoplasmosis and cryptococcosis, and create slip hazards. Professional pigeon control includes deterrent installation (spikes, netting, wire), dropping cleanup, and ongoing maintenance.

faqs.q: How much does pigeon control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Pigeon control in NYC varies by scope. Spike installation runs $15-$25 per linear foot. Bird netting costs $3-$10 per square foot. Full building pigeon management programs start at $1,000-$5,000. Dropping cleanup is quoted separately.

faqs.q: Are pigeon droppings dangerous?
faqs.a: Yes. Pigeon droppings carry over 60 diseases including histoplasmosis, cryptococcosis, and psittacosis. Dried droppings become airborne and can be inhaled. Professional cleanup with proper PPE is recommended, especially for large accumulations.

faqs.q: What is the most effective pigeon deterrent?
faqs.a: Bird netting is the most effective exclusion method for large areas. Spikes work well for ledges and sills. Electrical track systems deter without harming birds. We recommend a combination based on your specific building and pigeon behavior patterns.

faqs.q: Is it legal to kill pigeons in NYC?
faqs.a: Pigeons (rock doves) are not protected under federal or state law in New York. However, we focus on humane deterrent methods that prevent roosting and nesting rather than lethal control. This is more effective long-term.

faqs.q: How do I keep pigeons off my AC unit?
faqs.a: We install custom bird guard cages around AC units and HVAC equipment that prevent pigeons from roosting and nesting without restricting airflow. This also prevents pigeon droppings from clogging condensers.

licensingNote: All technicians are trained in bird management and hold relevant NYS licenses.
seoChecklist: Add service schema for bird control
competitorSigns: Competitors ranking for pigeon control searches

name: Bat Removal
description: Humane bat exclusion and removal services for residential and commercial properties.
extendedDescription: Several bat species inhabit the NYC metro area, roosting in attics, wall voids, and building exteriors. Bats are protected under NYS law, making professional exclusion the only legal removal method. Bat guano accumulation creates health hazards including histoplasmosis. Professional bat removal uses one-way exclusion devices and thorough entry point sealing.

faqs.q: How much does bat removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Bat exclusion in NYC costs $500-$2,500 depending on property size and number of entry points. Guano cleanup adds $500-$3,000 depending on accumulation. Full exclusion and cleanup typically runs $1,000-$4,000.

faqs.q: Is it legal to kill bats in New York?
faqs.a: No. Several bat species in New York are state-listed as threatened or endangered. All bat species are protected during maternity season (May-August). Professional exclusion using one-way devices is the only legal and humane removal method.

faqs.q: When can bats be excluded from a building?
faqs.a: Bat exclusion should only be done in early spring (March-April) or fall (September-November). Exclusion during maternity season (May-August) can trap flightless baby bats inside, which is illegal and inhumane.

faqs.q: Are bats dangerous?
faqs.a: Bats can carry rabies, and their guano harbors the fungus that causes histoplasmosis. Never handle a bat with bare hands. If a bat is found in a living space, contact us immediately for safe removal and consult your doctor about rabies exposure.

faqs.q: How do bats get into my house?
faqs.a: Bats can squeeze through gaps as small as 3/8 inch. Common entry points include ridge vents, soffit gaps, chimney flashing, and loose siding. A thorough inspection identifies all entry points before exclusion begins.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Nuisance Wildlife Control Operator licenses with bat handling certification.

name: Moth Control
description: Professional moth elimination for closets, pantries, and storage areas.
extendedDescription: Clothes moths and pantry moths (Indian meal moths) are common in NYC apartments, damaging expensive clothing, textiles, and stored food. Clothes moths target wool, silk, fur, and cashmere in closets and storage. Pantry moths infest grains, cereals, and dried goods. Professional treatment combines targeted applications, pheromone traps, and source elimination.

faqs.q: How much does moth control cost?
faqs.a: Moth treatment in NYC costs $150-$350 per treatment. Closet-focused clothes moth treatment runs $200-$350. Pantry moth treatment costs $150-$250 including inspection and source elimination.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have clothes moths or pantry moths?
faqs.a: Clothes moths are small, golden-tan, and avoid light. You'll find damage to wool, silk, and natural fiber clothing. Pantry moths are larger with brown/copper-banded wings and are found near stored food. Both leave behind larvae and webbing.

faqs.q: Can moths damage expensive clothing?
faqs.a: Yes. Clothes moth larvae feed on wool, cashmere, silk, fur, and other natural fibers. They can cause thousands of dollars in damage to a wardrobe. Professional treatment and preventive measures protect your investment.

faqs.q: How do pantry moths get into sealed food?
faqs.a: Pantry moths often enter homes already inside purchased food products. They can also chew through thin plastic and cardboard packaging. A thorough inspection of all stored food is the first step in elimination.

faqs.q: How long does moth treatment take to work?
faqs.a: Moth treatment reduces adult populations immediately. However, eggs and larvae take 2-4 weeks to fully cycle. We typically recommend a follow-up treatment at 3-4 weeks to ensure complete elimination.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Silverfish Control
description: Professional silverfish elimination and moisture control for NYC homes and businesses.
extendedDescription: Silverfish thrive in NYC's older buildings with high humidity, feeding on paper, glue, textiles, and starch. They're commonly found in bathrooms, basements, kitchens, and storage areas. While not dangerous, they can damage books, wallpaper, documents, and clothing. Treatment combines targeted applications with moisture reduction recommendations.

faqs.q: How much does silverfish treatment cost?
faqs.a: Silverfish treatment in NYC costs $125-$300. Treatment typically includes targeted applications in bathrooms, basements, and affected areas plus moisture recommendations. Follow-up treatments may be needed for severe infestations.

faqs.q: Are silverfish harmful?
faqs.a: Silverfish don't bite or carry diseases, but they damage paper, books, wallpaper, photographs, and clothing by feeding on starches and cellulose. Large populations can cause significant damage to stored items.

faqs.q: Why do I have silverfish in my bathroom?
faqs.a: Silverfish need high humidity to survive. Bathrooms, basements, and kitchens in NYC's older buildings provide ideal conditions. Reducing humidity with exhaust fans and dehumidifiers is key to long-term control.

faqs.q: Do silverfish mean I have a moisture problem?
faqs.a: Often yes. Silverfish thrive in 75-95% humidity. Their presence can indicate bathroom ventilation issues, plumbing leaks, or poor basement drainage. Addressing moisture is essential for permanent silverfish control.

faqs.q: How do I prevent silverfish from coming back?
faqs.a: Reduce humidity below 60%, use bathroom exhaust fans, fix any leaks, store papers and books in sealed containers, and maintain a regular treatment schedule. We provide detailed prevention plans for each property.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Centipede Control
description: Professional centipede and millipede elimination for NYC homes.
extendedDescription: House centipedes are one of the most common creepy-crawlies in NYC apartments. While they actually prey on other pests like roaches and spiders, many homeowners find them alarming. Their presence often indicates underlying moisture or pest issues. Treatment addresses centipedes directly while also targeting the prey insects that attract them.

faqs.q: How much does centipede control cost?
faqs.a: Centipede treatment in NYC costs $125-$275. Since centipedes often indicate other pest problems, comprehensive treatment addressing underlying issues may cost $200-$400.

faqs.q: Are house centipedes dangerous?
faqs.a: House centipedes can bite but rarely do, and their venom is not medically significant for most people. They're actually beneficial predators that eat cockroaches, spiders, and other pests. However, we understand most people don't want them in their home.

faqs.q: Why do I have so many centipedes?
faqs.a: Centipedes indicate two things: moisture and prey insects. They need high humidity and feed on other bugs. If you're seeing lots of centipedes, there's likely an underlying pest population and a moisture issue worth addressing.

faqs.q: Do centipedes mean I have other pests?
faqs.a: Usually yes. House centipedes are predators — they feed on cockroaches, silverfish, spiders, and other insects. A large centipede population suggests a healthy population of prey insects that should also be treated.

faqs.q: How do I keep centipedes out of my apartment?
faqs.a: Reduce humidity, seal cracks around baseboards and utility penetrations, use bathroom exhaust fans, and address underlying pest problems. Professional perimeter treatment creates a barrier that deters entry.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Cricket Control
description: Professional cricket elimination for basements, garages, and commercial properties.
extendedDescription: House crickets and camel crickets (spider crickets) are common in NYC basements, garages, and crawl spaces. House crickets create disruptive chirping, while camel crickets are silent but alarming in appearance. Both are attracted to moisture and can damage fabrics and papers. Treatment targets crickets and their harborage areas.

faqs.q: How much does cricket control cost?
faqs.a: Cricket control in NYC costs $125-$250. Basement-focused treatments for camel crickets run $150-$250. Preventive treatments to keep crickets from returning cost $30-$50/month.

faqs.q: What are those big jumping bugs in my basement?
faqs.a: Those are likely camel crickets (also called spider crickets or cave crickets). They're light brown, humpbacked, and can jump several feet. They thrive in dark, moist basements and are one of NYC's most common basement pests.

faqs.q: Are crickets harmful?
faqs.a: Crickets can damage fabrics, papers, and stored items by chewing on them. House crickets chirp loudly and can be extremely disruptive. Camel crickets don't chirp but their jumping behavior startles most people.

faqs.q: Why do I have crickets in my basement?
faqs.a: Crickets are attracted to moisture, darkness, and organic debris. NYC basements with humidity issues, storage clutter, and cracks in the foundation are ideal cricket habitat. Reducing moisture is key to long-term control.

faqs.q: Do crickets attract other pests?
faqs.a: Yes. Crickets are a food source for spiders, centipedes, and mice. Reducing the cricket population can help reduce these secondary pest problems as well.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Stink Bug Control
description: Professional stink bug prevention and removal for NYC homes and businesses.
extendedDescription: Brown marmorated stink bugs have become a major seasonal nuisance across the NYC metro area. They invade homes in fall seeking warmth and emerge in spring, releasing a foul odor when disturbed. Professional treatment focuses on perimeter exclusion and barrier applications before fall invasion season.

faqs.q: How much does stink bug control cost?
faqs.a: Stink bug treatment in NYC costs $125-$300. Preventive perimeter treatments before fall invasion season run $150-$250. Comprehensive exclusion with entry point sealing adds $300-$800.

faqs.q: When do stink bugs invade homes?
faqs.a: Stink bugs seek indoor shelter from September through November as temperatures drop. They're most noticeable on warm, sunny days when they cluster on south-facing walls. Preventive treatment in August-September is most effective.

faqs.q: Why do stink bugs smell so bad?
faqs.a: Stink bugs release a chemical compound (trans-2-decenal) from glands on their thorax as a defense mechanism when threatened or crushed. The smell is often described as cilantro or burnt tires. Avoid crushing them — vacuum them instead.

faqs.q: Can stink bugs damage my home?
faqs.a: Stink bugs don't cause structural damage or bite, but large numbers create a significant nuisance. Their odor can permeate living spaces, and they stain surfaces when crushed. They're primarily an agricultural pest that has adapted to urban environments.

faqs.q: How do I prevent stink bugs from getting in?
faqs.a: Seal cracks around windows, doors, siding, and utility penetrations. Repair damaged screens. Apply perimeter barrier treatment in late summer. These preventive measures are far more effective than treating after invasion.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Drain Fly Treatment
description: Professional drain fly elimination targeting breeding sites in drains and plumbing.
extendedDescription: Drain flies (moth flies) breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and sewage systems. They're extremely common in NYC apartments, restaurants, and commercial kitchens. While harmless, they multiply rapidly and indicate plumbing maintenance issues. Treatment requires identifying and cleaning breeding sites, not just killing adults.

faqs.q: How much does drain fly treatment cost?
faqs.a: Drain fly treatment in NYC costs $150-$300. Treatment includes drain inspection, bio-enzyme treatment, and adult fly elimination. Restaurants and commercial kitchens may require ongoing monthly programs at $75-$150/month.

faqs.q: What are drain flies?
faqs.a: Drain flies are small (1/8 inch), fuzzy, moth-like flies that breed in the organic slime inside drains. They're often seen hovering near bathroom and kitchen sinks, shower drains, and floor drains, especially in the morning.

faqs.q: Why do I keep getting drain flies?
faqs.a: Drain flies breed in the biofilm (organic buildup) inside pipes. Simply killing adult flies won't solve the problem — the breeding site must be cleaned. Drains that aren't used regularly are especially prone to buildup.

faqs.q: Can I get rid of drain flies with bleach?
faqs.a: Bleach provides temporary relief but doesn't eliminate the organic buildup where drain flies breed. Professional bio-enzyme treatments break down the biofilm that drain flies need to reproduce, providing lasting results.

faqs.q: Are drain flies common in NYC restaurants?
faqs.a: Very common. Floor drains, bar drains, and grease traps in NYC restaurants provide ideal breeding conditions. Regular drain maintenance and bio-enzyme treatments prevent drain fly issues and help with DOH compliance.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Pantry Pest Control
description: Professional elimination of pantry pests including Indian meal moths, beetles, and weevils.
extendedDescription: Pantry pests — Indian meal moths, flour beetles, grain weevils, and saw-toothed grain beetles — infest stored food products in NYC homes, restaurants, and grocery stores. They often arrive already inside purchased food products. Professional treatment involves thorough inspection, source identification, targeted treatment, and prevention recommendations.

faqs.q: How much does pantry pest control cost?
faqs.a: Pantry pest treatment in NYC costs $150-$300. This includes thorough inspection, source identification, treatment, and prevention recommendations. Commercial food storage facilities are quoted based on square footage.

faqs.q: Where do pantry pests come from?
faqs.a: Pantry pests most often arrive in purchased food products — flour, cereal, rice, pasta, dried fruit, nuts, and pet food. Eggs and larvae can be present in packaging before purchase. They then spread to other stored foods.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have pantry pests?
faqs.a: Signs include small moths flying near the kitchen, webbing in food packages, small beetles in flour or cereal, and tiny holes in food packaging. Check all stored grains, cereals, and dried goods for signs of infestation.

faqs.q: Do I need to throw away all my food?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. We'll inspect all stored food and identify which items are infested. Sealed, uninfested items can be saved. We recommend transferring all dry goods to airtight glass or hard plastic containers after treatment.

faqs.q: How do I prevent pantry pests?
faqs.a: Store all dry goods in airtight containers (not original packaging), inspect groceries before storing, rotate stock (use oldest first), keep pantry shelves clean, and consider freezing new flour/grain purchases for 72 hours before storing.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Fly Control
description: Professional fly control for homes, restaurants, and commercial properties across NYC.
extendedDescription: House flies, fruit flies, and cluster flies are persistent problems in NYC homes and restaurants. Flies carry bacteria and contaminate food, making them a health code concern for food service businesses. Professional fly control combines source elimination, exclusion, light traps, and targeted treatments for lasting results.

faqs.q: How much does fly control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Residential fly control costs $125-$250. Commercial fly management for restaurants starts at $150-$400 per visit, with monthly programs at $100-$200/month. Fly light trap installation is quoted separately.

faqs.q: How do I get rid of fruit flies?
faqs.a: Fruit flies breed in overripe produce, drains, and moist organic material. Professional treatment identifies all breeding sites, applies targeted treatments, and sets up monitoring traps. Eliminating the food source is essential.

faqs.q: Why do I have flies in my restaurant?
faqs.a: Restaurant flies typically breed in floor drains, garbage areas, recycling bins, and mop closets. NYC DOH requires active fly management in food service establishments. We provide DOH-compliant fly control programs.

faqs.q: Are flies a health code violation in NYC?
faqs.a: Yes. Active fly infestations in food service establishments can result in DOH violations and point deductions on health inspections. Regular professional fly management helps maintain compliance and protect your inspection grade.

faqs.q: What are cluster flies and why are they in my home?
faqs.a: Cluster flies are large, slow-moving flies that invade homes in fall and emerge on warm winter/spring days. They cluster in attics and wall voids. They don't breed indoors — they're overwintering. Exclusion and perimeter treatment prevents entry.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

name: Rodent Proofing
description: Comprehensive rodent exclusion and proofing services to permanently seal out rats and mice.
extendedDescription: Rodent proofing (exclusion) is the most effective long-term rodent control strategy. We inspect your entire property for entry points — gaps around pipes, utility lines, doors, vents, and foundation cracks — and seal them with rodent-proof materials like steel mesh, copper wool, and concrete. This prevents rats and mice from entering without relying solely on traps and baits.

faqs.q: How much does rodent proofing cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Rodent proofing in NYC costs $500-$3,000 depending on property size and number of entry points. A typical NYC apartment or townhouse runs $800-$1,500. Commercial properties and multi-unit buildings are quoted per property.

faqs.q: How is rodent proofing different from extermination?
faqs.a: Extermination kills existing rodents. Rodent proofing prevents new ones from entering. For lasting results, you need both — we eliminate the current population and then seal every entry point so they can't return.

faqs.q: What materials do you use for rodent proofing?
faqs.a: We use steel mesh, copper wool, hardware cloth, steel plates, concrete, and expanding foam reinforced with steel wool. Regular caulk and foam alone won't work — rats chew through them in hours.

faqs.q: How small of a gap can a mouse fit through?
faqs.a: Mice can squeeze through gaps as small as 1/4 inch (the diameter of a pencil). Rats can fit through gaps as small as 1/2 inch (the size of a quarter). We seal all gaps larger than 1/4 inch with rodent-proof materials.

faqs.q: Do you guarantee rodent proofing work?
faqs.a: Yes. We warranty our exclusion work and will return to address any new entry points at no charge during the warranty period. We also offer annual inspection programs to maintain your rodent-proof barrier.

licensingNote: All technicians hold NYS DEC Commercial Pesticide Applicator licenses.

faqs.q: How much does commercial pest control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Commercial pest control in NYC starts at $150-$400/month for small restaurants and offices. Larger facilities run $400-$1,500/month. Pricing depends on property size, pest pressure, and service frequency.

faqs.q: What is IPM (Integrated Pest Management)?
faqs.a: IPM is a comprehensive approach that combines prevention, monitoring, and targeted treatment to manage pests with minimal chemical use. NYC requires IPM for many commercial and institutional settings. We design IPM programs tailored to your facility.

faqs.q: Do you provide documentation for NYC health inspections?
faqs.a: Yes. We provide complete pest management documentation including service reports, pesticide application records, and bait station maps. This documentation helps maintain your DOH inspection grade.

faqs.q: How often should commercial properties be serviced?
faqs.a: Restaurants and food service: weekly or bi-weekly. Office buildings: monthly. Retail: monthly. Warehouses: monthly to quarterly. We customize frequency based on pest pressure and regulatory requirements.

faqs.q: Do you offer emergency commercial pest service?
faqs.a: Yes. We provide 24/7 emergency response for commercial accounts. Whether it's a pre-inspection emergency or an active infestation, we can have a technician on-site quickly.

faqs.q: How much does restaurant pest control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Restaurant pest control in NYC costs $200-$600/month depending on restaurant size and pest pressure. This includes regular service visits, monitoring, documentation, and emergency response. New client initial service starts at $400-$800.

faqs.q: Can pest violations affect my DOH letter grade?
faqs.a: Yes. Pest evidence is one of the most common critical violations on NYC DOH inspections. Live roaches, mouse droppings, or fly activity can result in significant point deductions and lower your letter grade from A to B or C.

faqs.q: Do you work around restaurant operating hours?

faqs.q: How often should my restaurant be serviced?
faqs.a: Most NYC restaurants need weekly or bi-weekly service. High-volume restaurants, those with basement prep areas, or those in older buildings may need weekly service. We assess your specific needs and recommend the right frequency.

faqs.q: How much does general pest control cost in NYC?

faqs.q: What pests does general pest control cover?
faqs.a: Our general service covers cockroaches, ants, spiders, silverfish, centipedes, crickets, and other common household insects. Specialized pests like bed bugs, termites, rodents, and wildlife require dedicated services.

faqs.q: How often should I get pest control in NYC?

faqs.q: Is regular pest control necessary in NYC?

faqs.q: Are your treatments safe for pets and children?

faqs.q: How much does emergency pest control cost?

faqs.q: How quickly can you respond to an emergency?
